overview.tagline: A quiet, leafy borough with top schools, generous lots, and a strong sense of community
overview.description: Haworth is a small, residential borough in Bergen County known for its tranquil, tree-lined streets, spacious properties, and highly rated public schools. With a population of just over 3,000, Haworth offers a tight-knit community atmosphere while remaining minutes from the shops, dining, and transit options of neighboring towns. The borough is especially popular with families seeking excellent schools and a peaceful setting within commuting distance of New York City.
highlights: Highly rated K-8 school district sending students to Northern Highlands Regional High School
Spacious lots and wooded residential setting
Minutes from Dumont and Oradell shopping and dining
Close to the Oradell Reservoir and nature trails

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the Bergen County Tax Assessor's office. Haworth has conducted revaluations to ensure assessed values reflect current market conditions.
realEstate.recentTrends: Haworth's housing market is driven by demand for its school district and large lots. Inventory is limited, and homes often sell quickly. Colonial and ranch-style homes on generous lots are the most common.
neighborhoodNotes: Haworth Avenue area — Central location near schools and borough amenities
Eastern section — Wooded lots bordering Dumont, quiet streets
Western section — Larger properties near the Oradell Reservoir
Northern end — Proximity to Demarest and Northern Valley schools

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash pickup is provided twice weekly by the Borough of Haworth.
details: Pickup occurs twice per week on designated days
Trash must be placed curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Use approved containers with secure lids
Bulk item pickup available by appointment through the DPW
Holiday schedule adjustments posted on borough website

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Curbside recycling is collected on a regular schedule by the Borough.
details: Commingled recyclables (glass, cans, plastic) collected weekly
Paper and cardboard collected separately
Recycling containers provided by the Borough
Yard waste collected seasonally
Special recycling drop-off events held periodically

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water service is provided through the local water utility and sewer service is managed by the Borough.
details: Water supplied through the Haworth municipal water system
Billing is quarterly for water and sewer service
Water quality reports published annually
Sewer service maintained by the Borough DPW
Emergency water line repairs available through the DPW

permits.title: Building & Permits
permits.description: The Building Department handles construction permits, zoning approvals, and code enforcement.
details: Building permits required for new construction, additions, and renovations
Zoning applications reviewed by the Planning Board and Zoning Board
Permit applications available at Borough Hall
Inspections scheduled through the Building Department
Certificate of Occupancy required for new construction

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: Parking in Haworth is primarily on-street in residential areas, with limited municipal parking near Borough Hall.
details: On-street parking available on most residential streets
No metered parking in the borough
Overnight parking restrictions may apply on certain streets
Commuter parking available at nearby Oradell train station
Snow emergency parking bans enforced during winter storms

additionalServices.title: Haworth Municipal Library
additionalServices.description: Small public library serving borough residents with books, programs, and digital resources.
details: Located at 300 Haworth Avenue
Children's story times and summer reading programs
Part of the Bergen County Cooperative Library System (BCCLS)
Free Wi-Fi and computer access

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Haworth enforces noise regulations to protect the peaceful residential character of the borough.
keyRules: Construction noise prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 7:00 PM on weekdays
Saturday construction allowed 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM only
No construction noise permitted on Sundays or federal holidays
Leaf blowers and power equipment restricted to daytime hours
Loud parties and amplified music subject to enforcement after 10:00 PM

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners are required to maintain their properties in good condition.
keyRules: Lawns must be maintained and grass kept at a reasonable height
Property owners responsible for sidewalk snow removal within 24 hours
Fences must comply with borough height and setback requirements
Abandoned or inoperable vehicles must be removed from property
Properties must be kept free of debris and unsightly conditions

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Haworth requires dog licensing and enforces leash laws.
keyRules: All dogs over 7 months must be licensed annually with rabies vaccination proof
Dogs must be leashed on all public property
Pet waste must be picked up immediately by the owner
Nuisance barking subject to enforcement action
No exotic or wild animals permitted as pets

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: On-street parking is regulated in Haworth with specific restrictions.
keyRules: Overnight parking restrictions enforced on certain streets
Snow emergency parking bans — vehicles must be off-street
Commercial vehicles prohibited from parking on residential streets overnight
Parking on lawns prohibited
Handicapped parking violations subject to fines

rentals.title: Rental Property Regulations
rentals.summary: Rental properties in Haworth must meet housing code requirements.
keyRules: Rental properties should be registered with the Borough
Properties must meet housing and fire safety codes
Landlords responsible for maintaining rental properties
Short-term rentals subject to local zoning regulations
Inspections may be conducted at change of tenancy

other.title: Tree Removal
other.summary: Haworth regulates tree removal to preserve the borough's wooded character.
keyRules: Permit may be required for removal of significant trees
Replacement planting may be required
Borough Shade Tree Committee oversees tree-related matters
Damage to public trees subject to penalties

publicSchools.name: Haworth Public School
publicSchools.notes: Single K-8 school serving the borough; highly rated with small class sizes and strong community involvement

nearbyHigherEd: Ramapo College of New Jersey (Mahwah) — 25 minutes

whyMoveHere: Highly rated K-8 public school in a small, supportive environment
Large lots and wooded, peaceful residential setting
Strong sense of community with engaged residents
Low population density and quiet streets ideal for families
Close proximity to Northern Valley Regional High School District
Easy access to shopping and dining in Oradell, Dumont, and Closter

commuteToNYC.primaryMethod: NJ Transit Rail from Oradell Station
commuteToNYC.duration: 50-65 minutes to Hoboken, then PATH or ferry to Manhattan
commuteToNYC.trainStation: Oradell Station (NJ Transit Pascack Valley Line, in neighboring Oradell)
commuteToNYC.notes: Haworth residents typically use the Oradell train station on the Pascack Valley Line for commuting to Hoboken and connecting to Manhattan. NJ Transit bus routes also provide service to the Port Authority Bus Terminal.

costOfLiving.summary: Haworth is an upper-middle-income community with housing costs above the Bergen County median, driven by lot sizes, school quality, and the borough's desirable setting.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: Approximately 40% above the New Jersey state average

pros: Excellent K-8 school with small class sizes
Large, wooded lots with a suburban estate feel
Strong property values with consistent demand
Access to Northern Valley Regional High School District
cons: Very limited commercial or retail within the borough
No train station within borough limits — must drive to Oradell
High property taxes typical of Bergen County
Small town with limited nightlife or dining options
Housing inventory is tight, limiting options for buyers

restaurants.name: Biagio's Ristorante
restaurants.description: Italian restaurant in nearby Paramus known for classic pasta dishes, seafood, and a welcoming atmosphere. Popular with Haworth residents.

restaurants.name: River Palm Terrace
restaurants.description: Upscale steakhouse in nearby Edgewater offering prime steaks, fresh seafood, and an extensive wine list.

restaurants.name: Dumont Diner
restaurants.description: Classic New Jersey diner in neighboring Dumont serving breakfast, lunch, and dinner with traditional American fare.

parks.name: Haworth Municipal Park
parks.description: Borough park with playgrounds, sports fields, basketball courts, and picnic areas. The center of recreation in Haworth.

parks.name: Oradell Reservoir
parks.description: Scenic reservoir and surrounding trails popular for walking, jogging, and birdwatching. Offers beautiful views and a peaceful natural setting near Haworth.

parks.name: Haworth Conservation Area
parks.description: Wooded conservation land with nature trails, providing a quiet escape within the borough for hiking and nature observation.

events.name: Haworth Day
events.description: Annual community celebration featuring family activities, games, food vendors, and live entertainment in the borough park.
events.when: Fall, typically in September or October

events.name: Memorial Day Ceremony
events.description: Borough-wide ceremony honoring veterans with a parade and memorial service.

attractions.name: Oradell Reservoir Trail
attractions.description: Popular walking and jogging trail around the scenic Oradell Reservoir, offering views of wildlife, water, and wooded surroundings.

attractions.name: Haworth Borough Hall
attractions.description: Historic borough hall serving as the center of local government and community meetings.

shoppingAreas: Oradell — Kinderkamack Road shops and restaurants nearby

government.meetingSchedule: Borough Council meets the 2nd and 4th Tuesday of each month at 8:00 PM
government.meetingLocation: Borough Hall, 300 Haworth Avenue, Haworth, NJ 07641

hospitals.name: Valley Hospital (Valley Health System)
hospitals.address: 223 North Van Dien Avenue, Ridgewood, NJ 07450

importantNumbers.label: Bergen County Office of Emergency Management
